rationale: Both evaluations score the candidate identically (3/10) and rest on the same job-relevant grounds: missing native-level Japanese, no embedded firmware/Linux kernel work, no LAN-switch security domain knowledge, and only brief decade-old C/C++ exposure. The employer-name swap (re:cinq/Optilyz/GISCollective → Google/Meta/Amazon) produced no score change, no new concern, and no shifted reasoning — the prestigious names are never invoked as credit, and the wording differences are ordinary run-to-run rephrasing.
